GDP per capita and life expectancy in 2020

This report uses Gapminder-style country panels for GDP per capita (gdp_pcap.csv) and life expectancy at birth (lex.csv), restricted to 2020.

Data structure

Each file is in wide form: geo (3-letter code), name, and one column per year. The tables below show the first five countries and a few year columns.

Sample: GDP per capita (first rows, selected years)

geo name 1800 1950 2000 2020
afg Afghanistan 560.8882 1728.976 1426.509 2769.686
ago Angola 435.2326 2490.898 5352.491 7556.968
alb Albania 547.5337 2169.303 6558.423 14662.796
and Andorra 1598.5313 9702.541 52814.695 55488.490
are UAE 1332.7771 1839.030 88047.227 65784.677

Sample: Life expectancy (first rows, selected years)

geo name 1800 1950 2000 2020
afg Afghanistan 28.21 49.1 61.2 64.6
ago Angola 26.98 40.8 54.0 62.8
alb Albania 35.40 55.1 75.1 77.4
and Andorra NA 70.0 80.8 82.7
are UAE 30.70 45.1 72.7 79.5

2020 subset

After dropping rows with missing 2020 values for either indicator, the analysis uses 193 countries with both GDP per capita and life expectancy in 2020.

World maps (2020)

Country values are matched to map polygons via maps::iso3166 (ISO alpha-3 to map region name) and ggplot2::map_data("world"). Unmatched or missing countries appear in light grey.

Relationship in 2020

Pearson correlation (on raw GDP values): 0.575.

Summary of paired 2020 observations

n median_gdp median_lex min_gdp max_gdp min_lex max_lex
193 14397.2 72.6 625.7 332201.8 53.8 85.7

Interpretation

In 2020, higher GDP per capita is associated with higher life expectancy across countries: the cloud of points slopes upward, especially on a log scale for income, and the fitted line on the log scale is clearly positive. Richer countries cluster toward the top-right; many low-income countries have lower life expectancy, though there is meaningful dispersion (public health, inequality, institutions, and recent shocks all move countries off a simple income–longevity line).

Reflection

Interacting with cursor was easy. I entered the prompts I needed to create the report with the dataset. The result is amazing and so much time was saved using this tool.
